nimbledeps/pkgs2/chronos-4.2.2-3a4c9477df8cef20a04e4f1b54a2d74fdfc2a3d0/chronos/internal/asyncfutures.nim
Line Branch Exec Source
1 #
2 # Chronos
3 #
4 # (c) Copyright 2015 Dominik Picheta

8 # Apache License, version 2.0, (LICENSE-APACHEv2)
9 # MIT license (LICENSE-MIT)
10
11 ## Features and utilities for `Future` that integrate it with the dispatcher
12 ## and the rest of the async machinery
13
14 {.push raises: [].}
15
16 import std/[sequtils, macros]
17 import stew/base10
18
19 import ./[asyncengine, raisesfutures]
20 import ../[config, futures]
21
22 export
23 raisesfutures.Raising, raisesfutures.InternalRaisesFuture,
24 raisesfutures.init, raisesfutures.error, raisesfutures.readError
25
26 when chronosStackTrace:
27 import std/strutils
28 when defined(nimHasStacktracesModule):
29 import system/stacktraces
30 else:
31 const
32 reraisedFromBegin = -10
33 reraisedFromEnd = -100
34
35 template LocCreateIndex*: auto {.deprecated: "LocationKind.Create".} =
36 LocationKind.Create
37 template LocFinishIndex*: auto {.deprecated: "LocationKind.Finish".} =
38 LocationKind.Finish
39 template LocCompleteIndex*: untyped {.deprecated: "LocationKind.Finish".} =
40 LocationKind.Finish
41
42 func `[]`*(loc: array[LocationKind, ptr SrcLoc], v: int): ptr SrcLoc {.
43 deprecated: "use LocationKind".} =
44 case v
45 of 0: loc[LocationKind.Create]
46 of 1: loc[LocationKind.Finish]
47 else: raiseAssert("Unknown source location " & $v)
48
49 type
50 FutureStr*[T] = ref object of Future[T]

52 gcholder*: string
53
54 FutureSeq*[A, B] = ref object of Future[A]
55 ## Deprecated

57
58 FuturePendingError* = object of FutureError
59 ## Error raised when trying to `read` a Future that is still pending
60 FutureCompletedError* = object of FutureError
61 ## Error raised when trying access the error of a completed Future
62
63 SomeFuture = Future|InternalRaisesFuture

66 noinline, noreturn, raises: FuturePendingError.} =

 206 template newFutureStr*[T](fromProc: static[string] = ""): FutureStr[T] {.deprecated.} =
128 ✗ ## Create a new future which can hold/preserve GC string until future will
129 ✗ ## not be completed.
130 ##
131 206 ## Specifying ``fromProc``, which is a string specifying the name of the proc
132 ## that this future belongs to, is a good habit as it helps with debugging.
133 newFutureStrImpl[T](getSrcLocation(fromProc))
134
135 proc done*(future: FutureBase): bool {.deprecated: "Use `completed` instead".} =
136 ## This is an alias for ``completed(future)`` procedure.
137 completed(future)
138
139 when chronosFutureTracking:
140 proc futureDestructor(udata: pointer) =
141 ## This procedure will be called when Future[T] got completed, cancelled or
142 ## failed and all Future[T].callbacks are already scheduled and processed.
143 let future = cast[FutureBase](udata)
144 if future == futureList.tail: futureList.tail = future.prev
145 if future == futureList.head: futureList.head = future.next
146 if not(isNil(future.next)): future.next.internalPrev = future.prev
147 if not(isNil(future.prev)): future.prev.internalNext = future.next
148 futureList.count.dec()
149
150 proc scheduleDestructor(future: FutureBase) {.inline.} =
151 callSoon(futureDestructor, cast[pointer](future))
152
153 2193 proc checkFinished(future: FutureBase, loc: ptr SrcLoc) =
154 ## Checks whether `future` is finished. If it is then raises a
155 ## ``FutureDefect``.
156 2193 if future.finished():
157 ✗ var msg = ""
158 ✗ msg.add("An attempt was made to complete a Future more than once. ")
159 ✗ msg.add("Details:")
160 ✗ msg.add("\n Future ID: " & Base10.toString(future.id))
161 ✗ msg.add("\n Creation location:")
162 ✗ msg.add("\n " & $future.location[LocationKind.Create])
163 ✗ msg.add("\n First completion location:")
164 ✗ msg.add("\n " & $future.location[LocationKind.Finish])
165 ✗ msg.add("\n Second completion location:")
166 ✗ msg.add("\n " & $loc)
167 when chronosStackTrace:
168 msg.add("\n Stack trace to moment of creation:")
169 msg.add("\n" & indent(future.stackTrace.strip(), 4))
170 msg.add("\n Stack trace to moment of secondary completion:")
171 msg.add("\n" & indent(getStackTrace().strip(), 4))
172 ✗ msg.add("\n\n")
173 ✗ var err = newException(FutureDefect, msg)
174 ✗ err.cause = future
175

261 16 proc tryCancel(future: FutureBase, loc: ptr SrcLoc): bool =
262 ✗ ## Perform an attempt to cancel ``future``.
263 ✗ ##
264 ✗ ## NOTE: This procedure does not guarantee that cancellation will actually
265 ## happened.
266 ✗ ##
267 ## Cancellation is the process which starts from the last ``future``
268 ## descendent and moves step by step to the parent ``future``. To initiate
269 ## this process procedure iterates through all non-finished ``future``
270 ## descendents and tries to find the last one. If last descendent is still
271 ## pending it will become cancelled and process will be initiated. In such
272 ## case this procedure returns ``true``.
273 ##
274 ## If last descendent future is not pending, this procedure will be unable to
275 ## initiate cancellation process and so it returns ``false``.
276
2/4
✗ Branch 0 not taken.
✓ Branch 1 taken 8 times.
✗ Branch 2 not taken.
✓ Branch 3 taken 8 times.
 8 if future.cancelled():
277 ✗ return true
278
2/4
✗ Branch 0 not taken.
✓ Branch 1 taken 8 times.
✗ Branch 2 not taken.
✓ Branch 3 taken 8 times.
 8 if future.finished():
279 ✗ return false
280

322 340 proc removeCallback*(future: FutureBase, cb: CallbackFunc,
323 udata: pointer) =
324 ## Remove future from list of callbacks - this operation may be slow if there

326 ✗ doAssert(not isNil(cb))
327 ✗ # Make sure to release memory associated with callback, or reference chains

332 ✗ future.internalCallbacks.keepItIf:

335 ✗ proc removeCallback*(future: FutureBase, cb: CallbackFunc) =

338 ✗ proc `callback=`*(future: FutureBase, cb: CallbackFunc, udata: pointer) {.
339 deprecated: "use addCallback/removeCallback/clearCallbacks to manage the callback list".} =
340 ## Clears the list of callbacks and sets the callback proc to be called when
341 ## the future completes.
342 ##
343 ## If future has already completed then ``cb`` will be called immediately.
344 ##
345 ## It's recommended to use ``addCallback`` or ``then`` instead.
346 # ZAH: how about `setLen(1); callbacks[0] = cb`
347 future.clearCallbacks
348 future.addCallback(cb, udata)
349
350 proc `callback=`*(future: FutureBase, cb: CallbackFunc) {.
351 deprecated: "use addCallback/removeCallback/clearCallbacks instead to manage the callback list".} =
352 ## Sets the callback proc to be called when the future completes.
353 ##
354 ## If future has already completed then ``cb`` will be called immediately.
355 {.push warning[Deprecated]: off.}
356 `callback=`(future, cb, cast[pointer](future))
357 {.pop.}
358
359 890 proc `cancelCallback=`*(future: FutureBase, cb: CallbackFunc) =
360 ## Sets the callback procedure to be called when the future is cancelled.
361 ##
362 ## This callback will be called immediately as ``future.cancel()`` invoked and
363 ## must be set before future is finished.
364
365 when chronosStrictFutureAccess:
366 doAssert not future.finished(),
367 "cancellation callback must be set before finishing the future"
368 future.internalCancelcb = cb

378 2215 proc futureContinue*(fut: FutureBase) {.raises: [], gcsafe.} =
379 # This function is responsible for calling the closure iterator generated by
380 # the `{.async.}` transformation either until it has completed its iteration
381 #
382 # Every call to an `{.async.}` proc is redirected to call this function
383 # instead with its original body captured in `fut.closure`.
384 2883 while true:
385 # Call closure to make progress on `fut` until it reaches `yield` (inside
386 # `await` typically) or completes / fails / is cancelled
387

413 when chronosStackTrace:
414 template getFilenameProcname(entry: StackTraceEntry): (string, string) =
415 when compiles(entry.filenameStr) and compiles(entry.procnameStr):
416 # We can't rely on "entry.filename" and "entry.procname" still being valid
417 # cstring pointers, because the "string.data" buffers they pointed to might
418 # be already garbage collected (this entry being a non-shallow copy,
419 # "entry.filename" no longer points to "entry.filenameStr.data", but to the
420 # buffer of the original object).
421 (entry.filenameStr, entry.procnameStr)
422 else:
423 ($entry.filename, $entry.procname)
424
425 proc `$`(stackTraceEntries: seq[StackTraceEntry]): string =
426 try:
427 when defined(nimStackTraceOverride) and declared(addDebuggingInfo):
428 let entries = addDebuggingInfo(stackTraceEntries)
429 else:
430 let entries = stackTraceEntries
431
432 # Find longest filename & line number combo for alignment purposes.
433 var longestLeft = 0
434 for entry in entries:
435 let (filename, procname) = getFilenameProcname(entry)
436
437 if procname == "": continue
438
439 let leftLen = filename.len + len($entry.line)
440 if leftLen > longestLeft:
441 longestLeft = leftLen
442
443 var indent = 2
444 # Format the entries.
445 for entry in entries:
446 let (filename, procname) = getFilenameProcname(entry)
447
448 if procname == "":
449 if entry.line == reraisedFromBegin:
450 result.add(spaces(indent) & "#[\n")
451 indent.inc(2)
452 elif entry.line == reraisedFromEnd:
453 indent.dec(2)
454 result.add(spaces(indent) & "]#\n")
455 continue
456
457 let left = "$#($#)" % [filename, $entry.line]
458 result.add((spaces(indent) & "$#$# $#\n") % [
459 left,
460 spaces(longestLeft - left.len + 2),
461 procname
462 ])
463 except ValueError as exc:
464 return exc.msg # Shouldn't actually happen since we set the formatting
465 # string
466
467 proc injectStacktrace(error: ref Exception) =
468 const header = "\nAsync traceback:\n"
469
470 var exceptionMsg = error.msg
471 if header in exceptionMsg:
472 # This is messy: extract the original exception message from the msg
473 # containing the async traceback.
474 let start = exceptionMsg.find(header)
475 exceptionMsg = exceptionMsg[0..<start]
476
477 var newMsg = exceptionMsg & header
478
479 let entries = getStackTraceEntries(error)
480 newMsg.add($entries)
481
482 newMsg.add("Exception message: " & exceptionMsg & "\n")
483
484 # # For debugging purposes
485 # newMsg.add("Exception type:")
486 # for entry in getStackTraceEntries(future.error):
487 # newMsg.add "\n" & $entry
488 error.msg = newMsg
489
490 proc deepLineInfo(n: NimNode, p: LineInfo) =
491 n.setLineInfo(p)
492 for i in 0..<n.len:
493 deepLineInfo(n[i], p)
494
495 macro internalRaiseIfError*(fut: FutureBase, info: typed) =
496 # Check the error field of the given future and raise if it's set to non-nil.
497 # This is a macro so we can capture the line info from the original call and
498 # report the correct line number on exception effect violation
499 let
500 info = info.lineInfoObj()
501 res = quote do:
502 if not(isNil(`fut`.internalError)):
503 when chronosStackTrace:
504 injectStacktrace(`fut`.internalError)
505 raise `fut`.internalError
506 res.deepLineInfo(info)
507 res
508
509 macro internalRaiseIfError*(fut: InternalRaisesFuture, raises, info: typed) =
510 # For InternalRaisesFuture[void, (ValueError, OSError), will do:
511 # {.cast(raises: [ValueError, OSError]).}:
512 # if isNil(f.error): discard
513 # else: raise f.error
514 # TODO https://github.com/nim-lang/Nim/issues/22937
515 # we cannot `getTypeInst` on the `fut` - when aliases are involved, the
516 # generics are lost - so instead, we pass the raises list explicitly
517
518 let
519 info = info.lineInfoObj()
520 types = getRaisesTypes(raises)
521
522 if isNoRaises(types):
523

573 58 proc read*[T: not void](fut: Future[T] ): lent T {.raises: [CatchableError].} =

585 proc read*(fut: Future[void]) {.raises: [CatchableError].} =
586 ## Checks that `fut` completed.
587 ##
588 ## If the future failed or was cancelled, the corresponding exception will be
589 ## raised.
590 ##
591 ## If the future is still pending, `FuturePendingError` will be raised.
592 if not fut.finished():
593 raiseFuturePendingError(fut)
594
595 internalRaiseIfError(fut, fut)
596
597 proc readError*(fut: FutureBase): ref CatchableError {.raises: [FutureError].} =
598 ## Retrieves the exception of the failed or cancelled `fut`.
599 ##
600 ## If the future was completed with a value, `FutureCompletedError` will be
601 ## raised.
602 ##
603 ## If the future is still pending, `FuturePendingError` will be raised.
604 if not fut.finished():
605 raiseFuturePendingError(fut)
606
607 if isNil(fut.error):
608 raiseFutureCompletedError(fut)
609
610 fut.error
611
612 template taskFutureLocation(future: FutureBase): string =
613

 562 ## Spawns a new concurrent async task.
669 562 ##
670 158 ## Tasks may not raise exceptions or be cancelled - a ``Defect`` will be
671 232 ## raised when this happens.
672 256 ##
673 ## This should be used instead of ``discard`` and ``asyncCheck`` when calling
674 ## an ``async`` procedure without ``await``, to ensure exceptions in the
675 ## returned future are not silently discarded.
676 ##
677 ## Note, that if passed ``future`` is already finished, it will be checked
678 ## and processed immediately.

693 proc asyncCheck*[T](future: Future[T]) {.
694 deprecated: "Raises Defect on future failure, fix your code and use" &
695 " asyncSpawn!".} =
696 ## This function used to raise an exception through the `poll` call if
697 ## the given future failed - there's no way to handle such exceptions so this
698 ## function is now an alias for `asyncSpawn`
699 ##
700 when T is void:
701 asyncSpawn(future)
702 else:
703 proc cb(data: pointer) =
704 if future.failed():
705 raise newException(FutureDefect, taskErrorMessage(future))
706 elif future.cancelled():
707 raise newException(FutureDefect, taskCancelMessage(future))
708
709 if not(future.finished()):
710 # We adding completion callback only if ``future`` is not finished yet.
711 future.addCallback(cb)
712 else:
713 cb(nil)
714
715 proc asyncDiscard*[T](future: Future[T]) {.
716 deprecated: "Use asyncSpawn or `discard await`".} = discard
717 ## `asyncDiscard` will discard the outcome of the operation - unlike `discard`
718 ## it also throws away exceptions! Use `asyncSpawn` if you're sure your
719 ## code doesn't raise exceptions, or `discard await` to ignore successful
720 ## outcomes
721
722 proc `and`*[T, Y](fut1: Future[T], fut2: Future[Y]): Future[void] {.
723 deprecated: "Use allFutures[T](varargs[Future[T]])".} =
724 ## Returns a future which will complete once both ``fut1`` and ``fut2``
725 ## finish.
726 ##
727 ## If cancelled, ``fut1`` and ``fut2`` futures WILL NOT BE cancelled.
728 var retFuture = newFuture[void]("chronos.`and`")
729 proc cb(data: pointer) =
730 if not(retFuture.finished()):
731 if fut1.finished() and fut2.finished():
732 if cast[pointer](fut1) == data:
733 if fut1.failed():
734 retFuture.fail(fut1.error)
735 else:
736 retFuture.complete()
737 else:
738 if fut2.failed():
739 retFuture.fail(fut2.error)
740 else:
741 retFuture.complete()
742 fut1.addCallback(cb)
743 fut2.addCallback(cb)
744
745 proc cancellation(udata: pointer) =
746 # On cancel we remove all our callbacks only.
747 if not(fut1.finished()):
748 fut1.removeCallback(cb)
749 if not(fut2.finished()):
750 fut2.removeCallback(cb)
751
752 retFuture.cancelCallback = cancellation
753 return retFuture
754
755 template orImpl*[T, Y](fut1: Future[T], fut2: Future[Y]): untyped =
756 var cb: proc(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe, raises: [].}
757 cb = proc(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe, raises: [].} =
758 if not(retFuture.finished()):
759 var fut = cast[FutureBase](udata)
760 if cast[pointer](fut1) == udata:
761 fut2.removeCallback(cb)
762 else:
763 fut1.removeCallback(cb)
764 if fut.failed():
765 retFuture.fail(fut.error, warn = false)
766 else:
767 retFuture.complete()
768
769 proc cancellation(udata: pointer) =
770 # On cancel we remove all our callbacks only.
771 if not(fut1.finished()):
772 fut1.removeCallback(cb)
773 if not(fut2.finished()):
774 fut2.removeCallback(cb)
775
776 if fut1.finished():
777 if fut1.failed():
778 retFuture.fail(fut1.error, warn = false)
779 else:
780 retFuture.complete()
781 return retFuture
782
783 if fut2.finished():
784 if fut2.failed():
785 retFuture.fail(fut2.error, warn = false)
786 else:
787 retFuture.complete()
788 return retFuture
789
790 fut1.addCallback(cb)
791 fut2.addCallback(cb)
792
793 retFuture.cancelCallback = cancellation
794 retFuture
795
796 proc `or`*[T, Y](fut1: Future[T], fut2: Future[Y]): Future[void] =
797 ## Returns a future which will complete once either ``fut1`` or ``fut2``
798 ## finish.
799 ##
800 ## If ``fut1`` or ``fut2`` future is failed, the result future will also be
801 ## failed with an error stored in ``fut1`` or ``fut2`` respectively.
802 ##
803 ## If both ``fut1`` and ``fut2`` future are completed or failed, the result
804 ## future will depend on the state of ``fut1`` future. So if ``fut1`` future
805 ## is failed, the result future will also be failed, if ``fut1`` future is
806 ## completed, the result future will also be completed.
807 ##
808 ## If cancelled, ``fut1`` and ``fut2`` futures WILL NOT BE cancelled.
809 var retFuture = newFuture[void]("chronos.or()")
810 orImpl(fut1, fut2)
811
812
813 proc all*[T](futs: varargs[Future[T]]): auto {.
814 deprecated: "Use allFutures(varargs[Future[T]])".} =
815 ## Returns a future which will complete once all futures in ``futs`` finish.
816 ## If the argument is empty, the returned future completes immediately.
817 ##
818 ## If the awaited futures are not ``Future[void]``, the returned future
819 ## will hold the values of all awaited futures in a sequence.
820 ##
821 ## If the awaited futures *are* ``Future[void]``, this proc returns
822 ## ``Future[void]``.
823 ##
824 ## Note, that if one of the futures in ``futs`` will fail, result of ``all()``
825 ## will also be failed with error from failed future.
826 ##
827 ## TODO: This procedure has bug on handling cancelled futures from ``futs``.
828 ## So if future from ``futs`` list become cancelled, what must be returned?
829 ## You can't cancel result ``retFuture`` because in such way infinite
830 ## recursion will happen.
831 let totalFutures = len(futs)
832 var completedFutures = 0
833
834 # Because we can't capture varargs[T] in closures we need to create copy.
835 var nfuts = @futs
836
837 when T is void:
838 var retFuture = newFuture[void]("chronos.all(void)")
839 proc cb(udata: pointer) =
840 if not(retFuture.finished()):
841 inc(completedFutures)
842 if completedFutures == totalFutures:
843 for nfut in nfuts:
844 if nfut.failed():
845 retFuture.fail(nfut.error)
846 break
847 if not(retFuture.failed()):
848 retFuture.complete()
849
850 for fut in nfuts:
851 fut.addCallback(cb)
852
853 if len(nfuts) == 0:
854 retFuture.complete()
855
856 return retFuture
857 else:
858 var retFuture = newFuture[seq[T]]("chronos.all(T)")
859 var retValues = newSeq[T](totalFutures)
860
861 proc cb(udata: pointer) =
862 if not(retFuture.finished()):
863 inc(completedFutures)
864 if completedFutures == totalFutures:
865 for k, nfut in nfuts:
866 if nfut.failed():
867 retFuture.fail(nfut.error)
868 break
869 else:
870 retValues[k] = nfut.value
871 if not(retFuture.failed()):
872 retFuture.complete(retValues)
873
874 for fut in nfuts:
875 fut.addCallback(cb)
876
877 if len(nfuts) == 0:
878 retFuture.complete(retValues)
879
880 return retFuture
881
882 proc oneIndex*[T](futs: varargs[Future[T]]): Future[int] {.
883 deprecated: "Use one[T](varargs[Future[T]])".} =
884 ## Returns a future which will complete once one of the futures in ``futs``
885 ## complete.
886 ##
887 ## If the argument is empty, the returned future FAILS immediately.
888 ##
889 ## Returned future will hold index of completed/failed future in ``futs``
890 ## argument.
891 var nfuts = @futs
892 var retFuture = newFuture[int]("chronos.oneIndex(T)")
893
894 proc cb(udata: pointer) =
895 var res = -1
896 if not(retFuture.finished()):
897 var rfut = cast[FutureBase](udata)
898 for i in 0..<len(nfuts):
899 if cast[FutureBase](nfuts[i]) != rfut:
900 nfuts[i].removeCallback(cb)
901 else:
902 res = i
903 retFuture.complete(res)
904
905 for fut in nfuts:
906 fut.addCallback(cb)
907
908 if len(nfuts) == 0:
909 retFuture.fail(newException(ValueError, "Empty Future[T] list"))
910
911 return retFuture
912
913 proc oneValue*[T](futs: varargs[Future[T]]): Future[T] {.
914 deprecated: "Use one[T](varargs[Future[T]])".} =
915 ## Returns a future which will finish once one of the futures in ``futs``
916 ## finish.
917 ##
918 ## If the argument is empty, returned future FAILS immediately.
919 ##
920 ## Returned future will hold value of completed ``futs`` future, or error
921 ## if future was failed.
922 var nfuts = @futs
923 var retFuture = newFuture[T]("chronos.oneValue(T)")
924
925 proc cb(udata: pointer) =
926 var resFut: Future[T]
927 if not(retFuture.finished()):
928 var rfut = cast[FutureBase](udata)
929 for i in 0..<len(nfuts):
930 if cast[FutureBase](nfuts[i]) != rfut:
931 nfuts[i].removeCallback(cb)
932 else:
933 resFut = nfuts[i]
934 if resFut.failed():
935 retFuture.fail(resFut.error)
936 else:
937 when T is void:
938 retFuture.complete()
939 else:
940 retFuture.complete(resFut.read())
941
942 for fut in nfuts:
943 fut.addCallback(cb)
944
945 if len(nfuts) == 0:
946 retFuture.fail(newException(ValueError, "Empty Future[T] list"))
947
948 return retFuture
949

955 8 ## NOTE: Compared to the `tryCancel()` call, this procedure call guarantees

958 6 proc checktick(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe, raises: [].} =

1043 608 ## Perform cancellation ``future`` return Future which will be completed when
1044 ## ``future`` become finished (completed with value, failed or cancelled).

1046 ## NOTE: Compared to the `tryCancel()` call, this procedure call guarantees
1047 ## that ``future``will be finished (completed with value, failed or cancelled)
1048 ## as quickly as possible.

 2 cancelAndWait(getSrcLocation(), FutureBase(future))
1059
1060 template cancelAndWait*(futs: varargs[FutureBase]): Future[void].Raising([]) =
1061 ## Perform cancellation of all the ``futs``. Returns Future which will be
1062 ## completed when all the ``futs`` become finished (completed with value,
1063 ## failed or cancelled).
1064 ##
1065 ## NOTE: Compared to the `tryCancel()` call, this procedure call guarantees
1066 ## that all the ``futs``will be finished (completed with value, failed or
1067 ## cancelled) as quickly as possible.
1068 ##
1069 ## NOTE: It is safe to pass finished futures in ``futs`` (completed with
1070 ## value, failed or cancelled).
1071 ##
1072 ## NOTE: If ``futs`` is an empty array, procedure returns completed Future.
1073 cancelAndWait(getSrcLocation(), futs)
1074
1075 template cancelAndWait*(futs: openArray[SomeFuture]): Future[void].Raising([]) =
1076 ## Perform cancellation of all the ``futs``. Returns Future which will be
1077 ## completed when all the ``futs`` become finished (completed with value,
1078 ## failed or cancelled).
1079 ##
1080 ## NOTE: Compared to the `tryCancel()` call, this procedure call guarantees
1081 ## that all the ``futs``will be finished (completed with value, failed or
1082 ## cancelled) as quickly as possible.
1083 ##
1084 ## NOTE: It is safe to pass finished futures in ``futs`` (completed with
1085 ## value, failed or cancelled).
1086 ##
1087 ## NOTE: If ``futs`` is an empty array, procedure returns completed Future.
1088 cancelAndWait(getSrcLocation(), futs)
1089
1090 proc noCancel*[F: SomeFuture](future: F): auto = # async: (raw: true, raises: asyncraiseOf(future) - CancelledError
1091 ## Prevent cancellation requests from propagating to ``future`` while
1092 ## forwarding its value or error when it finishes.
1093 ##
1094 ## This procedure should be used when you need to perform operations which
1095 ## should not be cancelled at all cost, for example closing sockets, pipes,
1096 ## connections or servers. Usually it become useful in exception or finally
1097 ## blocks.
1098 when F is InternalRaisesFuture:
1099 type
1100 E = F.E
1101 InternalRaisesFutureRaises = E.remove(CancelledError)
1102
1103 let retFuture = newFuture[F.T]("chronos.noCancel(T)",
1104 {FutureFlag.OwnCancelSchedule})
1105 template completeFuture() =
1106 const canFail = when declared(InternalRaisesFutureRaises):
1107 InternalRaisesFutureRaises isnot void
1108 else:
1109 true
1110
1111 if future.completed():
1112 when F.T is void:
1113 retFuture.complete()
1114 else:
1115 retFuture.complete(future.value)
1116 else:
1117 when canFail: # Avoid calling `failed` on non-failing raises futures
1118 if future.failed():
1119 retFuture.fail(future.error, warn = false)
1120 else:
1121 raiseAssert("Unexpected future state [" & $future.state & "]")
1122 else:
1123 raiseAssert("Unexpected future state [" & $future.state & "]")
1124
1125 proc continuation(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe.} =
1126 completeFuture()
1127
1128 if future.finished():
1129 completeFuture()
1130 else:
1131 retFuture.cancelCallback = nil
1132 future.addCallback(continuation)
1133 retFuture
1134
1135 proc allFutures*(futs: varargs[FutureBase]): Future[void] {.
1136 async: (raw: true, raises: [CancelledError]).} =
1137 ## Returns a future which will complete only when all futures in ``futs``
1138 ## will be completed, failed or cancelled.
1139 ##
1140 ## If the argument is empty, the returned future COMPLETES immediately.
1141 ##
1142 ## On cancel all the awaited futures ``futs`` WILL NOT BE cancelled.
1143 let retFuture = newFuture[void]("chronos.allFutures()")
1144
1145 var pending = futs.filterIt(not it.finished())
1146
1147 if pending.len == 0:
1148 retFuture.complete()
1149 return retFuture
1150
1151 var finishedFutures = 0
1152 proc cb(udata: pointer) =
1153 if not(retFuture.finished()):
1154 inc(finishedFutures)
1155 if finishedFutures == pending.len:
1156 retFuture.complete()
1157 reset(pending)
1158
1159 proc cancellation(udata: pointer) =
1160 # On cancel we remove all our callbacks only.
1161 for fut in pending:
1162 if not(fut.finished()):
1163 fut.removeCallback(cb)
1164 reset(pending)
1165
1166 for fut in pending:
1167 fut.addCallback(cb)
1168
1169 retFuture.cancelCallback = cancellation
1170
1171 retFuture
1172
1173 proc allFutures*[T](futs: varargs[Future[T]]): Future[void] {.
1174 async: (raw: true, raises: [CancelledError]).} =
1175 ## Returns a future which will complete only when all futures in ``futs``
1176 ## will be completed, failed or cancelled.
1177 ##
1178 ## If the argument is empty, the returned future COMPLETES immediately.
1179 ##
1180 ## On cancel all the awaited futures ``futs`` WILL NOT BE cancelled.
1181 # Because we can't capture varargs[T] in closures we need to create copy.
1182 allFutures(futs.mapIt(FutureBase(it)))
1183
1184 proc allFutures*[T, E](futs: varargs[InternalRaisesFuture[T, E]]): Future[void] {.
1185 async: (raw: true, raises: [CancelledError]).} =
1186 ## Returns a future which will complete only when all futures in ``futs``
1187 ## will be completed, failed or cancelled.
1188 ##
1189 ## If the argument is empty, the returned future COMPLETES immediately.
1190 ##
1191 ## On cancel all the awaited futures ``futs`` WILL NOT BE cancelled.
1192 # Because we can't capture varargs[T] in closures we need to create copy.
1193 allFutures(futs.mapIt(FutureBase(it)))
1194

1237 template oneImpl: untyped =
1238 # If one of the Future[T] already finished we return it as result
1239 when declared(fut0):
1240 if fut0.finished():
1241 retFuture.complete(fut0)
1242 return retFuture
1243
1244 for fut in futs:
1245 if fut.finished():
1246 retFuture.complete(fut)
1247 return retFuture
1248
1249 # Because we can't capture openArray/varargs in closures we need to create copy.
1250 var nfuts = newSeqOfCap[typeof(futs[0])](ord(declared(fut0)) + futs.len)
1251
1252 when declared(fut0):
1253 nfuts.add fut0
1254 nfuts.add futs
1255
1256 var cb: proc(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe, raises: [].}
1257 cb = proc(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe, raises: [].} =
1258 if not(retFuture.finished()):
1259 for fut in nfuts.mitems():
1260 if cast[pointer](fut) == udata:
1261 retFuture.complete(move(fut))
1262 else:
1263 fut.removeCallback(cb)
1264 reset(nfuts)
1265 reset(cb)
1266
1267 proc cancellation(udata: pointer) =
1268 # On cancel we remove all our callbacks only.
1269 for fut in nfuts:
1270 fut.removeCallback(cb)
1271 reset(nfuts)
1272 reset(cb)
1273
1274 for fut in nfuts:
1275 fut.addCallback(cb, cast[pointer](fut))
1276

1280
1281 proc one*(fut0: SomeFuture, futs: varargs[SomeFuture]): Future[SomeFuture] {.
1282 async: (raw: true, raises: [CancelledError]).} =
1283 ## Waits for one of the given futures to finish and returns it.
1284 ##
1285 ## If any of the futures is already finished, returns immediately.
1286 ##
1287 ## On success returned Future will hold the finished Future[T].
1288 ##
1289 ## On cancel futures in ``futs`` WILL NOT BE cancelled.
1290 ##
1291 ## This function is a synonym for `race`.
1292 let retFuture = newFuture[SomeFuture]("chronos.one()")
1293
1294 oneImpl
1295
1296 proc one*(futs: openArray[SomeFuture]): Future[SomeFuture] {.
1297 async: (raw: true, raises: [ValueError, CancelledError]).} =
1298 ## Waits for one of the given futures to finish and returns it.
1299 ##
1300 ## If any of the futures is already finished, returns immediately.
1301 ##
1302 ## If the argument is empty, a `ValueError` is raised.
1303 ##
1304 ## On success returned Future will hold the finished Future[T].
1305 ##
1306 ## On cancel futures in ``futs`` WILL NOT BE cancelled.
1307 ##
1308 ## This function is a synonym for `race`.
1309 let retFuture = newFuture[SomeFuture]("chronos.one()")
1310
1311 if len(futs) == 0:
1312 retFuture.fail(newException(ValueError, "Empty Future[T] list"))
1313 return retFuture
1314
1315 oneImpl
1316
1317 proc race*(fut0: FutureBase, futs: varargs[FutureBase]): Future[FutureBase] {.
1318 async: (raw: true, raises: [CancelledError]).} =
1319 ## Waits for one of the given futures to finish and returns it.
1320 ##
1321 ## If any of the futures is already finished, returns immediately.
1322 ##
1323 ## On success returned Future will hold the finished FutureBase.
1324 ##
1325 ## On cancel futures in ``futs`` WILL NOT BE cancelled.
1326 let retFuture = newFuture[FutureBase]("chronos.race()")

1446 proc stepsAsync*(number: int): Future[void] {.
1447 async: (raw: true, raises: [CancelledError]).} =
1448 ## Suspends the execution of the current async procedure for the next
1449 ## ``number`` of asynchronous steps (``poll()`` calls).
1450 ##
1451 ## This primitive can be useful when you need to create more deterministic
1452 ## tests and cases.
1453 doAssert(number > 0, "Number should be positive integer")
1454 var
1455 retFuture = newFuture[void]("chronos.stepsAsync(int)")
1456 counter = 0
1457 continuation: proc(data: pointer) {.gcsafe, raises: [].}
1458
1459 continuation = proc(data: pointer) {.gcsafe, raises: [].} =
1460 if not(retFuture.finished()):
1461 inc(counter)
1462 if counter < number:
1463 internalCallTick(continuation)
1464 else:
1465 retFuture.complete()
1466
1467 if number <= 0:
1468 retFuture.complete()
1469 else:
1470 internalCallTick(continuation)
1471
1472 retFuture
1473
1474 proc idleAsync*(): Future[void] {.
1475 async: (raw: true, raises: [CancelledError]).} =
1476 ## Suspends the execution of the current asynchronous task until "idle" time.
1477 ##
1478 ## "idle" time its moment of time, when no network events were processed by
1479 ## ``poll()`` call.
1480 var retFuture = newFuture[void]("chronos.idleAsync()")
1481
1482 proc continuation(data: pointer) {.gcsafe.} =
1483 if not(retFuture.finished()):
1484 retFuture.complete()
1485
1486 proc cancellation(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe.} =
1487 discard
1488
1489 retFuture.cancelCallback = cancellation
1490 callIdle(continuation, nil)
1491 retFuture

1493

1568 17
1569 proc withTimeout*[T](fut: Future[T], timeout: int): Future[bool] {.
1570 inline, deprecated: "Use withTimeout(Future[T], Duration)".} =
1571 withTimeout(fut, timeout.milliseconds())
1572
1573 proc waitUntilImpl[F: SomeFuture](fut: F, retFuture: auto,
1574 deadline: auto): auto =
1575 var timeouted = false
1576
1577 template completeFuture(fut: untyped, timeout: bool): untyped =
1578 if fut.failed():
1579 retFuture.fail(fut.error(), warn = false)
1580 elif fut.cancelled():
1581 if timeout:
1582 # Its possible that `future` could be cancelled in some other place. In
1583 # such case we can't detect if it was our cancellation due to timeout,
1584 # or some other cancellation.
1585 retFuture.fail(newException(AsyncTimeoutError, "Timeout exceeded!"))
1586 else:
1587 retFuture.cancelAndSchedule()
1588 else:
1589 when type(fut).T is void:
1590 retFuture.complete()
1591 else:
1592 retFuture.complete(fut.value)
1593
1594 proc continuation(udata: pointer) {.raises: [].} =
1595 if not(retFuture.finished()):
1596 if timeouted:
1597 # When timeout is exceeded and we cancelled future via cancelSoon(),
1598 # its possible that future at this moment already has value
1599 # and/or error.
1600 fut.completeFuture(timeouted)
1601 return
1602 if not(fut.finished()):
1603 timeouted = true
1604 fut.cancelSoon()
1605 else:
1606 fut.completeFuture(false)
1607
1608 var cancellation: proc(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe, raises: [].}
1609 cancellation = proc(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe, raises: [].} =
1610 deadline.removeCallback(continuation)
1611 if not(fut.finished()):
1612 fut.cancelSoon()
1613 else:
1614 fut.completeFuture(false)
1615
1616 if fut.finished():
1617 fut.completeFuture(false)
1618 else:
1619 if deadline.finished():
1620 retFuture.fail(newException(AsyncTimeoutError, "Timeout exceeded!"))
1621 else:
1622 retFuture.cancelCallback = cancellation
1623 fut.addCallback(continuation)
1624 deadline.addCallback(continuation)
1625 retFuture
1626
1627 proc waitImpl[F: SomeFuture](fut: F, retFuture: auto, timeout: Duration): auto =
1628 var
1629 moment: Moment
1630 timer: TimerCallback
1631 timeouted = false
1632
1633 template completeFuture(fut: untyped, timeout: bool): untyped =
1634 if fut.failed():
1635 retFuture.fail(fut.error(), warn = false)
1636 elif fut.cancelled():
1637 if timeout:
1638 retFuture.fail(newException(AsyncTimeoutError, "Timeout exceeded!"))
1639 else:
1640 retFuture.cancelAndSchedule()
1641 else:
1642 when type(fut).T is void:
1643 retFuture.complete()
1644 else:
1645 retFuture.complete(fut.value)
1646
1647 proc continuation(udata: pointer) {.raises: [].} =
1648 if not(retFuture.finished()):
1649 if timeouted:
1650 # We should not unconditionally fail `retFuture` with
1651 # `AsyncTimeoutError`. Initiated by timeout handler cancellation
1652 # could fail, in this case we could get `fut` in complete or in failed
1653 # state, so we should return error/value instead of `AsyncTimeoutError`.
1654 fut.completeFuture(timeouted)
1655 return
1656 if not(fut.finished()):
1657 # Timer exceeded first.
1658 timeouted = true
1659 fut.cancelSoon()
1660 else:
1661 # Future `fut` completed/failed/cancelled first.
1662 if not(isNil(timer)):
1663 clearTimer(timer)
1664 fut.completeFuture(false)
1665 timer = nil
1666
1667 var cancellation: proc(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe, raises: [].}
1668 cancellation = proc(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe, raises: [].} =
1669 if not(fut.finished()):
1670 if not(isNil(timer)):
1671 clearTimer(timer)
1672 fut.cancelSoon()
1673 else:
1674 fut.completeFuture(false)
1675
1676 timer = nil
1677
1678 if fut.finished():
1679 fut.completeFuture(false)
1680 else:
1681 if timeout.isZero():
1682 retFuture.fail(newException(AsyncTimeoutError, "Timeout exceeded!"))
1683 elif timeout.isInfinite():
1684 retFuture.cancelCallback = cancellation
1685 fut.addCallback(continuation)
1686 else:
1687 moment = Moment.fromNow(timeout)
1688 retFuture.cancelCallback = cancellation
1689 timer = setTimer(moment, continuation, nil)
1690 fut.addCallback(continuation)
1691
1692 retFuture
1693
1694 proc wait*[T](fut: Future[T], timeout = InfiniteDuration): Future[T] =
1695 ## Returns a future which will complete once future ``fut`` completes
1696 ## or if timeout of ``timeout`` milliseconds has been expired.
1697 ##
1698 ## If ``timeout`` is ``-1``, then statement ``await wait(fut)`` is
1699 ## equal to ``await fut``.
1700 ##
1701 ## TODO: In case when ``fut`` got cancelled, what result Future[T]
1702 ## should return, because it can't be cancelled too.
1703 var
1704 retFuture = newFuture[T]("chronos.wait(duration)",
1705 {FutureFlag.OwnCancelSchedule})
1706 # We set `OwnCancelSchedule` flag, because we going to cancel `retFuture`
1707 # manually at proper time.
1708
1709 waitImpl(fut, retFuture, timeout)
1710
1711 proc wait*[T](fut: Future[T], timeout = -1): Future[T] {.
1712 inline, deprecated: "Use wait(Future[T], Duration)".} =
1713 if timeout == -1:
1714 wait(fut, InfiniteDuration)
1715 elif timeout == 0:
1716 wait(fut, ZeroDuration)
1717 else:
1718 wait(fut, timeout.milliseconds())
1719
1720 proc wait*[T](fut: Future[T], deadline: SomeFuture): Future[T] =
1721 ## Returns a future which will complete once future ``fut`` completes
1722 ## or if ``deadline`` future completes.
1723 ##
1724 ## If `deadline` future completes before future `fut` -
1725 ## `AsyncTimeoutError` exception will be raised.
1726 ##
1727 ## Note: `deadline` future will not be cancelled and/or failed.
1728 ##
1729 ## Note: While `waitUntil(future)` operation is pending, please avoid any
1730 ## attempts to cancel future `fut`. If it happens `waitUntil()` could
1731 ## introduce undefined behavior - it could raise`CancelledError` or
1732 ## `AsyncTimeoutError`.
1733 ##
1734 ## If you need to cancel `future` - cancel `waitUntil(future)` instead.
1735 var
1736 retFuture = newFuture[T]("chronos.wait(future)",
1737 {FutureFlag.OwnCancelSchedule})
1738 # We set `OwnCancelSchedule` flag, because we going to cancel `retFuture`
1739 # manually at proper time.
1740 waitUntilImpl(fut, retFuture, deadline)
1741
1742 proc join*(future: FutureBase): Future[void] {.
1743 async: (raw: true, raises: [CancelledError]).} =
1744 ## Returns a future which will complete once future ``future`` completes.
1745 ##
1746 ## This primitive helps to carefully monitor ``future`` state, in case of
1747 ## cancellation ``join`` operation it will not going to cancel ``future``.
1748 ##
1749 ## If ``future`` is already completed - ``join`` will return completed
1750 ## future immediately.
1751 let retFuture = newFuture[void]("chronos.join()")
1752
1753 proc continuation(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe.} =
1754 retFuture.complete()
1755
1756 proc cancellation(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe.} =
1757 future.removeCallback(continuation, cast[pointer](retFuture))
1758
1759 if not(future.finished()):
1760 future.addCallback(continuation, cast[pointer](retFuture))
1761 retFuture.cancelCallback = cancellation
1762 else:
1763 retFuture.complete()
1764
1765 retFuture
1766
1767 proc join*(future: SomeFuture): Future[void] {.
1768 async: (raw: true, raises: [CancelledError]).} =
1769 ## Returns a future which will complete once future ``future`` completes.
1770 ##
1771 ## This primitive helps to carefully monitor ``future`` state, in case of
1772 ## cancellation ``join`` operation it will not going to cancel ``future``.
1773 join(FutureBase(future))
1774
1775 when defined(windows):
1776 import ../osdefs
1777
1778 proc waitForSingleObject*(handle: HANDLE,
1779 timeout: Duration): Future[WaitableResult] {.
1780 async: (raises: [AsyncError, CancelledError], raw: true).} =
1781 ## Waits until the specified object is in the signaled state or the
1782 ## time-out interval elapses. WaitForSingleObject() for asynchronous world.
1783 let flags = WT_EXECUTEONLYONCE
1784
1785 var
1786 retFuture = newFuture[WaitableResult]("chronos.waitForSingleObject()")
1787 waitHandle: WaitableHandle = nil
1788
1789 proc continuation(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe.} =
1790 doAssert(not(isNil(waitHandle)))
1791 if not(retFuture.finished()):
1792 let
1793 ovl = cast[PtrCustomOverlapped](udata)
1794 returnFlag = WINBOOL(ovl.data.bytesCount)
1795 res = closeWaitable(waitHandle)
1796 if res.isErr():
1797 retFuture.fail(newException(AsyncError, osErrorMsg(res.error())))
1798 else:
1799 if returnFlag == FALSE:
1800 retFuture.complete(WaitableResult.Ok)
1801 else:
1802 retFuture.complete(WaitableResult.Timeout)
1803
1804 proc cancellation(udata: pointer) {.gcsafe.} =
1805 doAssert(not(isNil(waitHandle)))
1806 if not(retFuture.finished()):
1807 discard closeWaitable(waitHandle)
1808
1809 let wres = uint32(waitForSingleObject(handle, DWORD(0)))
1810 if wres == WAIT_OBJECT_0:
1811 retFuture.complete(WaitableResult.Ok)
1812 return retFuture
1813 elif wres == WAIT_ABANDONED:
1814 retFuture.fail(newException(AsyncError, "Handle was abandoned"))
1815 return retFuture
1816 elif wres == WAIT_FAILED:
1817 retFuture.fail(newException(AsyncError, osErrorMsg(osLastError())))
1818 return retFuture
1819
1820 if timeout == ZeroDuration:
1821 retFuture.complete(WaitableResult.Timeout)
1822 return retFuture
1823
1824 waitHandle =
1825 block:
1826 let res = registerWaitable(handle, flags, timeout, continuation, nil)
1827 if res.isErr():
1828 retFuture.fail(newException(AsyncError, osErrorMsg(res.error())))
1829 return retFuture
1830 res.get()
1831
1832 retFuture.cancelCallback = cancellation
1833 return retFuture
1834
1835 {.pop.} # Automatically deduced raises from here onwards
1836
1837 87 proc readFinished[T: not void; E](fut: InternalRaisesFuture[T, E]): lent T =
1838 87 internalRaiseIfError(fut, E, fut)
1839 fut.internalValue
1840
1841 proc read*[T: not void, E](fut: InternalRaisesFuture[T, E]): lent T = # {.raises: [E, FuturePendingError].}
1842 ## Retrieves the value of `fut`.
1843 ##
1844 ## If the future failed or was cancelled, the corresponding exception will be
1845 ## raised.
1846 ##
1847 ## If the future is still pending, `FuturePendingError` will be raised.
1848 if not fut.finished():
1849 raiseFuturePendingError(fut)
1850
1851 fut.readFinished()
1852
1853 proc read*[E](fut: InternalRaisesFuture[void, E]) = # {.raises: [E].}
1854 ## Checks that `fut` completed.
1855 ##
1856 ## If the future failed or was cancelled, the corresponding exception will be
1857 ## raised.
1858 ##
1859 ## If the future is still pending, `FuturePendingError` will be raised.
1860 if not fut.finished():
1861 raiseFuturePendingError(fut)
1862
1863 internalRaiseIfError(fut, E, fut)
1864
1865 174 proc waitFor*[T: not void; E](fut: InternalRaisesFuture[T, E]): lent T = # {.raises: [E]}
1866 ## Blocks the current thread of execution until `fut` has finished, returning
1867 ## its value.
1868 ##
1869 ## If the future failed or was cancelled, the corresponding exception will be
1870 ## raised.
1871 ##
1872 ## Must not be called recursively (from inside `async` procedures).
1873 ##
1874 ## See also `await`, `Future.read`
1875

1897 proc wait*(fut: InternalRaisesFuture, timeout = InfiniteDuration): auto =
1898 type
1899 T = type(fut).T
1900 E = type(fut).E
1901 InternalRaisesFutureRaises = E.prepend(CancelledError, AsyncTimeoutError)
1902
1903 let
1904 retFuture = newFuture[T]("chronos.wait(duration)", {OwnCancelSchedule})
1905 # We set `OwnCancelSchedule` flag, because we going to cancel `retFuture`
1906 # manually at proper time.
1907
1908 waitImpl(fut, retFuture, timeout)

1910 proc wait*(fut: InternalRaisesFuture, deadline: SomeFuture): auto =
1911 type
1912 T = type(fut).T
1913 E = type(fut).E
1914 InternalRaisesFutureRaises = E.prepend(CancelledError, AsyncTimeoutError)
1915
1916 let
1917 retFuture = newFuture[T]("chronos.wait(future)", {OwnCancelSchedule})
1918 # We set `OwnCancelSchedule` flag, because we going to cancel `retFuture`
1919 # manually at proper time.
1920
1921 waitUntilImpl(fut, retFuture, deadline)
